title: Infosys Clarifies DGGI GST Refund Show Cause Notice of Rs 414.88 Crores
detailed_summary: Infosys clarified media reports about a Directorate General of GST Intelligence show cause notice concerning alleged ineligible GST input tax credit refunds. DGGI sought information in May 2025, issued a pre-show cause on 30 th  July 2025, and then issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties. The notice alleges that services provided by Infosys's overseas branches are not export of services and that refunds claimed were erroneous. Infosys assessed the merits, obtained external tax and legal advice, and filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka on 19 th  September 2025 challenging the legitimacy of the notice. Infosys stated there is no tax demand as on date and no material impact from the media article.

key_facts.fact: DGGI had in May 2025 sought information on the GST refunds claimed by Infosys.
DGGI issued a pre-show cause on 30 th  July 2025, after which Infosys sought additional information, relevant documents, and time to file a response.
DGGI issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties.
The show cause notice alleges that services provided by Infosys's overseas branches are not export of services and that the refund claimed by the Company is allegedly erroneous.
Infosys filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka on 19 th  September 2025 challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice.
Infosys stated that there is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.
Infosys stated that there is no material impact of the article on the Company.
